suite: EPIC 2E-J ENV-B1A-R PART 11 — Virtual-Origin helper static/functional self-test

results.test: Path normalization: legitimate paths (including "/" -> index.html) resolve to the expected project-relative path
Traversal rejection: every required traversal path (literal, encoded, double-encoded, backslash, drive-letter, null-byte) is rejected
results.evidence: [{"path":"/../secret.txt","result":{"ok":false,"reason":"parent-directory traversal segment rejected"}},{"path":"/%2e%2e/secret.txt","result":{"ok":false,"reason":"parent-directory traversal segment rejected"}},{"path":"/qa/../../secret.txt","result":{"ok":false,"reason":"parent-directory traversal segment rejected"}},{"path":"/qa/%2e%2e/%2e%2e/secret.txt","result":{"ok":false,"reason":"parent-directory traversal segment rejected"}},{"path":"/qa%2f..%2fsecret.txt","result":{"ok":false,"reason":"parent-directory traversal segment rejected"}},{"path":"/qa%5c..%5csecret.txt","result":{"ok":false,"reason":"backslash (literal or encoded) rejected"}},{"path":"/C:%5csecret.txt","result":{"ok":false,"reason":"backslash (literal or encoded) rejected"}},{"path":"/%00secret.txt","result":{"ok":false,"reason":"null byte present after decoding"}},{"path":"/%252e%252e/secret.txt","result":{"ok":false,"reason":"parent-directory traversal segment rejected"}},{"path":"/qa/..%2f..%2fsecret.txt","result":{"ok":false,"reason":"parent-directory traversal segment rejected"}}]

results.test: resolveSafeLocalPath: a legitimate path resolves strictly inside projectRoot
resolveSafeLocalPath: a traversal attempt is rejected before any filesystem access
decodeFullyOrNull: malformed percent-encoding degrades to null (never throws)
decodeFullyOrNull: double-encoded traversal fully decodes to ".."
MIME mapping: all 13 required extensions map to their exact required MIME type
MIME mapping: an unknown extension falls back to application/octet-stream
MIME mapping: extension matching is case-insensitive
Origin matching: the origin root itself matches
Origin matching: a path under the origin matches
Origin matching: a different host does NOT match (e.g. a prefix-confusable host)
Origin matching: localhost never matches the virtual origin
Google Fonts classification: fonts.googleapis.com stylesheet requests are recognized
Google Fonts classification: fonts.gstatic.com asset requests are recognized
Google Fonts classification: an unrelated host is never misclassified as Google Fonts
External-host classification: a genuinely external host is flagged unexpected
External-host classification: the virtual origin itself is never flagged unexpected
External-host classification: Google Fonts hosts are never flagged unexpected (they are stubbed, not aborted-and-logged)
Localhost/private-IP detection: localhost is flagged
Localhost/private-IP detection: 127.0.0.1 is flagged
Localhost/private-IP detection: a private 192.168.x.x address is flagged
Localhost/private-IP detection: the virtual origin itself is never flagged
qa/helpers/playwright-virtual-origin.mjs is readable
qa/playwright-virtual-origin-smoke.mjs is readable
No localhost/127.0.0.1 string is ever used as the navigation target — navigation goes only to the virtual origin
No local HTTP server is constructed anywhere in the harness (no http.createServer / .listen(port))
Neither harness file imports "node:http"
Both harness files reference the exact virtual origin "http://lumixa.test"
The smoke test creates its BrowserContext with serviceWorkers: "block"
The helper installs exactly ONE universal route handler (never multiple context.route() calls that could shadow each other via LIFO matching)
The helper module never imports "playwright" itself (only operates on an already-created context) — statically testable even when the package is unavailable
The smoke test never attempts to download a Browser (no "playwright install" / "npx playwright" invocation)

disclaimer: This suite calls the helper's real exported pure functions directly (path safety, MIME mapping, origin/external/Google-Fonts classification) and audits the two harness files' source text. It does not launch a Browser and proves nothing about actual Playwright/Chromium behavior — that is the separate runtime smoke test's job.
